schema_notes: Anchor facts only. JSON shape, field names, ranking methodology and filenames are the executor's choice — the grader checks semantic content + roughly correct numbers (±20% on dynamic dims), not exact key naming. Q1 2026 (2026-01-01 .. 2026-03-31) traction snapshot for FIVE LLM agent frameworks, fetched live on 2026-05-02.

expected_winner.rationale: Tops all 5 traction signals (PRs, releases, issues, HN stories, SO questions). The unanimous top-1 finding is the central result the user should derive.

secondary_findings.highest_team_breadth_rationale: samuelcolvin (pydantic founder) alone has 300+ public repos.
secondary_findings.highest_pr_to_issue_ratio_rationale: 256 merged PRs vs 130 issues opened = ~1.97 — the team is shipping faster than complaints arrive.

cross_framework_dep_overlap.method_hint: Fetch each framework's canonical runtime pyproject.toml (langchain monorepo: libs/langchain/pyproject.toml; langgraph monorepo: libs/langgraph/pyproject.toml; crewAI workspace: lib/crewai/pyproject.toml; pydantic-ai: pydantic_ai_slim/pyproject.toml; llama_index: root pyproject.toml + llama-index-core/pyproject.toml unioned). Extract names from PEP-621 [project] dependencies = [...] and/or [tool.poetry.dependencies]. Strip version specifiers, lowercase, dedupe. Then compute set overlap across the 5 frameworks.
cross_framework_dep_overlap.ecosystem_hub_rationale: pydantic is the only Python package depended on by ALL 5 LLM agent frameworks (langchain, llama_index, crewAI, pydantic-ai, langgraph). It functions as the universal data-validation / structured-output substrate of the LLM agent ecosystem. httpx and pyyaml are second-tier hubs (in 3 of 5 each).
cross_framework_dep_overlap.tolerance: Dep lists drift over time as frameworks publish new versions. The grader checks: (a) the executor actually fetched a dep list per framework (file existence in trace, OR per-framework dependencies array in the data); (b) `pydantic` appears in the shared/hub set; (c) the hub is identified explicitly. The exact contents of `shared_in_3plus_frameworks` may shift slightly (e.g. httpx added/removed as a transitive becomes/stops being a direct dep) but pydantic-as-universal-hub is the stable property.

tolerances.q1_merged_prs: ±20% (immutable historical count, but tolerance absorbs methodology differences in date-window inclusivity / query construction)
tolerances.q1_releases: ±20% (immutable historical count, but tolerance absorbs date-window edge cases on releases)
tolerances.so_q1_question_count: ±5 absolute (Stack Exchange search/advanced is sensitive to query phrasing; for sparse results absolute tolerance is more appropriate)
tolerances.top3_contributors: set equality with at-least-2-of-3 logins matching expected (contributor rankings drift slowly)
tolerances.team_breadth_score: ±50% absolute; the rank ordering (pydantic-ai having the highest team breadth) is the stable property and is checked instead of absolute values
